2025-11-01.title: After Days of Protests, Tanzania’s President Is Declared Election Winner
2025-11-01.abstract: Election monitors and members of the European Parliament have questioned the election’s integrity, and violent protests have rocked the country.

2025-11-01.title: Carney Says He Apologized to Trump Over Ad Reviving Reagan’s Tariff Criticism
2025-11-01.abstract: The Canadian prime minister also said that he had asked the province of Ontario not to air the ad that later prompted the president to end trade talks.

2025-11-01.title: Working Past 100? In Japan, Some People Never Quit.
2025-11-01.abstract: We met five remarkable people who’ve lived for more than a century and have yet to retire. For them, a working life is a richer one.

2025-11-01.title: He Stayed in Belarus for His Imprisoned Wife. Now He’s Locked Up, Too.
2025-11-01.abstract: Two journalists, both in detention for their work, show how President Aleksandr Lukashenko’s repressive machine grinds on despite warming ties with the U.S.

2025-11-01.title: Saudi Prince Plans Ambitious U.S. Visit, but Opening Ties With Israel Is Unlikely
2025-11-01.abstract: The crown prince of Saudi Arabia is pushing for a security pact and a nuclear deal with the United States yet is not expected to recognize Israel soon, despite President Trump’s wishes.

2025-11-02.title: 2 Arrested in Britain After Stabbing on Train
2025-11-02.abstract: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain denounced the attack, which the police said left nine victims with “life-threatening injuries.”

2025-11-02.title: Fire at Discount Store in Northern Mexico Kills at Least 23
2025-11-02.abstract: The fire ripped through a store in Hermosillo, a city in Sonora State. Officials said minors were among the victims.

2025-11-02.title: These Cats Do It for Glory, Not Salmon. (No, It’s the Salmon.)
2025-11-02.abstract: Speed eating contests are part of a growing list of pet-related events in Thailand that also include competitions for skateboarding dogs and felines that look like cows.

2025-11-02.title: An Outlier in Irish Politics Has Become the Country’s President
2025-11-02.abstract: Propelled by anti-establishment fervor, Catherine Connolly was elected last week with a landmark popular mandate.

2025-11-02.title: Communities in Hurricane-Ravaged Jamaica Still Cut Off as Death Toll Climbs
2025-11-02.abstract: The authorities have yet to reach dozens of areas in Jamaica, raising the question of how many people really died in last week’s storm.

2025-11-03.title: Another Deadly Quake, and a Cascade of Calamities for Afghanistan
2025-11-03.abstract: A 6.3-magnitude earthquake struck near Mazar-i-Sharif, a northern city known for its magnificent Blue Mosque, which suffered damage.

2025-11-03.title: Arrests in Louvre Heist Show Power of DNA Databases in Solving Crimes
2025-11-03.abstract: France’s trove of DNA profiles has helped solve high-profile crimes and was used to find some of the Louvre suspects, and it is growing. The police can also access other countries’ databases.

2025-11-03.title: With ‘a Billion’ Eyes on Them, India’s Women Lift Cricket World Cup
2025-11-03.abstract: A team that faced seemingly insurmountable odds wins its first championship, with wider ramifications for the role women play in public life.

2025-11-03.title: The Alps Are Melting, but the Villagers Will Not Be Moved
2025-11-03.abstract: Switzerland is racing to rebuild Blatten, which was crushed by a glacier. It’s a sign of the economic and emotional costs of a warming Europe.

2025-11-03.title: A Celebrated French Writer Loved Russia. War Forced a Reckoning.
2025-11-03.abstract: Emmanuel Carrère’s best sellers on Russia grew out of a deep affection. Since Moscow invaded Ukraine, he has traveled to the war-torn country to rethink his views.

2025-11-04.title: What Trade War? China’s Export Juggernaut Marches On
2025-11-04.abstract: As Trump has imposed steep tariffs on China, American importers are buying much less. But China has offset the decline from the United States with breathtaking speed.

2025-11-04.title: Kim Yong-nam, Longtime Ceremonial Leader of North Korea, Dies at 97
2025-11-04.abstract: In a country where political purges are frequent, Mr. Kim was a notable exception and served three generations of its dynastic rulers.

2025-11-04.title: Dozens Killed After Typhoon Kalmaegi Brings Flooding to Central Philippines
2025-11-04.abstract: Many of the victims drowned, the authorities said. Hundreds of thousands of people had evacuated before Typhoon Kalmaegi hit on Tuesday.

2025-11-04.title: Save the Amazon or Drill for Oil? Brazil Says It Can Do Both.
2025-11-04.abstract: Brazil, which is hosting the 30th U.N. Climate Change Conference this month, wants to show the world it’s a leader in safeguarding the planet. Its record tells a more complicated story.

2025-11-04.title: In Some Parts of Scotland, ‘The Only Thing We Need Is People’
2025-11-04.abstract: Bucking the anti-immigration trend in British politics, remote areas of Scotland would like to attract foreign workers to offset declining local populations.

2025-11-05.title: Scars in the Blue Mosque Reveal Afghanistan’s Rifts
2025-11-05.abstract: The namesake of Mazar-i-Sharif suffered countless cracks in a 6.3-magnitude quake, but, like Afghanistan, it has endured for centuries.

2025-11-05.title: ‘Strung Up and Tortured’: Hostage Recounts 2 ½ Years of Captivity
2025-11-05.abstract: Elizabeth Tsurkov, a citizen of Israel and Russia, recounts a harrowing story of cruelty, survival, U.S. diplomatic pressure and, finally, release from the grip of a group backed by Iran.

2025-11-05.title: What to Know About the Militia That Kidnapped Elizabeth Tsurkov
2025-11-05.abstract: Kataib Hezbollah, a hard-line Iraqi militia linked to Iran, held Elizabeth Tsurkov, a citizen of Israel and Russia, hostage for more than two years.

2025-11-05.title: Bookstores on Edge as Kremlin Sets Sights on Policing Books
2025-11-05.abstract: Restrictions on publishers and sellers have grown more severe. Volumes are being pulled from shelves or redacted like secret documents, but bookstores remain important sources of community.

2025-11-05.title: Fire at Retirement Home in Bosnia Kills at Least 11
2025-11-05.abstract: The country’s prime minister called the blaze “a disaster of enormous proportions.” The cause was still to be determined.

2025-11-06.title: Remains of the Last American Hostage Held in Gaza Returned to Israel
2025-11-06.abstract: Israeli officials told Itay Chen’s family last year that he was probably killed on Oct. 7, 2023, but relatives put off mourning until his body had come home.

2025-11-06.title: In China, Victims of Abuse Are Told to ‘Keep It in the Family’
2025-11-06.abstract: Cases of domestic violence in China point to a legal system that looks good on paper but is failing victims because of a lack of resources and political will.

2025-11-06.title: ‘Broken My Hope’: Trump’s Move to Slash Refugee Arrivals Ricochets Widely
2025-11-06.abstract: President Trump’s policy has shut the door on all but a tiny fraction of people across the world seeking refuge in the United States from conflict, persecution or both.

2025-11-06.title: Philippines Faces Grim Typhoon Aftermath as Another Big Storm Looms
2025-11-06.abstract: The death toll for Typhoon Kalmaegi rose into the triple digits, and the country is bracing for another cyclone expected this weekend.

2025-11-06.title: At Least One Dead After Boiler Tower at Power Plant in South Korea Collapses
2025-11-06.abstract: The structure was being demolished when it collapsed. One person was killed, two were rescued, and several more were believed to still be buried.

2025-11-07.title: U.N. Security Council Removes Syria’s President From Sanctions List
2025-11-07.abstract: The decision comes days before the Syrian president, Ahmed al-Shara, is expected in Washington for the first time since he came to power.

2025-11-07.title: Former King of Spain, Juan Carlos, Details Death of Brother in Memoir
2025-11-07.abstract: He wrote about the accident that killed his brother nearly 70 years ago. The book also describes his respect for Gen. Francisco Franco, the former dictator.

2025-11-07.title: China’s New Aircraft Carrier Signals Naval Ambitions
2025-11-07.abstract: The Fujian, China’s most advanced carrier, went into official service this week. It brings the country closer to challenging U.S. naval dominance.

2025-11-07.title: Seven Years for Antiwar Stickers? Russian Activist Would Do It Again.
2025-11-07.abstract: Freed in a major prisoner swap, Aleksandra Skochilenko said “the values of freedom of speech, of peace, could be more important than spending even 10 years in jail.”

2025-11-07.title: A Canadian Factory Town Blames Trump’s Tariffs for ‘a Punch to the Gut’
2025-11-07.abstract: An automaker’s decision to shift Canadian jobs to the United States has left workers in Brampton, Ontario, feeling betrayed and angry.

2025-11-08.title: Canada Culls Hundreds of Ostriches as a Court and a Kennedy Fail to Save Them
2025-11-08.abstract: The birds, exposed to the avian flu, were killed after Canada’s Supreme Court refused to hear an appeal and a rescue effort by Robert F. Kennedy Jr. fell short.

2025-11-08.title: What I Learned From the ‘New Globalists’ of an Optimistic Nation
2025-11-08.abstract: Few countries are excited about globalization anymore, but Vietnam is still into it — wholeheartedly. Do the reasons go beyond economic growth?

2025-11-08.title: In China, the Dream of Outrunning Time
2025-11-08.abstract: Longevity labs, “immortality islands” and grapeseed pills are part of China’s national project to conquer aging, despite sometimes shaky science and extravagant claims.

2025-11-08.title: Tornado Rips Through Southern Brazil, Killing at Least 5
2025-11-08.abstract: Pounding rain and winds of over 150 miles per hour left a trail of devastation in the state of Paraná.

2025-11-08.title: ‘You Are All Terrorists’: Four Months in a Salvadoran Prison
2025-11-08.abstract: The Times interviewed dozens of migrant men sent to a prison in El Salvador by the Trump administration. Independent forensic analysts called the testimony credible and consistent and said the treatment met the U.N.’s definition of torture.

2025-11-09.title: Spain’s True-Crime Capital Is Fed Up
2025-11-09.abstract: A decades-old killing in a tiny village in a northern mountain town has turned the village into a destination for true-crime enthusiasts, creating a headache for remaining residents.

2025-11-09.title: Philippines, Still Reeling From One Typhoon, Is Hit by Another
2025-11-09.abstract: Typhoon Fung-wong made landfall on the main island of Luzon, prompting the evacuation of more than one million people, just days after an earlier storm killed over 200.

2025-11-09.title: Israeli Academics Find Themselves Isolated Despite Gaza Cease-Fire
2025-11-09.abstract: Boycotts of Israeli universities, largely imposed in Europe, have multiplied since the start of the war and reflect Israel’s international isolation over its conduct in Gaza.

2025-11-09.title: Japan Lifts Tsunami Advisory After Strong Earthquake Off Its Coast
2025-11-09.abstract: A 6.7-magnitude quake struck off Japan’s coast early Sunday evening, prompting a tsunami advisory, which was later lifted.

2025-11-09.title: A Quebec Writer Confronts His ‘Little Darkness’ as a Class Defector
2025-11-09.abstract: Jean-Philippe Pleau’s book and play about moving up socially became a cultural reckoning in Quebec, but created a gulf with his family.

2025-11-10.title: Second Migrant Child Dies on ‘Reverse Migration’ Boat Route
2025-11-10.abstract: A 3-year-old from Colombia died when a boat carrying migrants back to South America capsized off Panama’s Caribbean Coast, an official said. Another child drowned on the same migrant route in February.

2025-11-10.title: Hundreds of Migrants Missing Off Malaysia’s Coast
2025-11-10.abstract: A boat said to be carrying people from the Rohingya ethnic minority capsized, and another was missing. At least seven bodies were recovered.

2025-11-10.title: ‘Gandalf’ the Medical Marijuana Grower: Folk Hero or Criminal?
2025-11-10.abstract: A police raid and criminal case against a longtime cultivator of cannabis in New Zealand’s Northland region has stirred up debates about medicinal marijuana.

2025-11-10.title: ‘Like It Was the End of the World’: A Million Flee From Typhoon in the Philippines
2025-11-10.abstract: Grabbing children and leaving their homes behind, residents evacuated before Typhoon Fung-wong hit.

2025-11-10.title: He Was Known for Kleptocratic Rule and Bloodshed. Now He’s a National Hero.
2025-11-10.abstract: Indonesia’s president bestowed the honor on the dictator Suharto, who died in 2008, in what many said was a stunning move of revisionist history.

2025-11-11.title: India Is Investigating a Deadly Car Explosion. Here’s What to Know.
2025-11-11.abstract: The car exploded near a metro station in a historic part of New Delhi on Monday night. Officials are investigating and security is tight around the region.

2025-11-11.title: As Iraqis Vote for a Parliament, U.S. Presses to Rid Country of Iran’s Influence
2025-11-11.abstract: After a U.S. occupation, years of sectarian violence and a jihadist insurgency, Iraq has become an improbable haven of calm in the Middle East.

2025-11-11.title: How China Reached Into New York to Stop a Tiny Film Festival
2025-11-11.abstract: A showcase for independent Chinese films was scrapped after the Chinese authorities pressured directors, moderators and even a volunteer to pull out.

2025-11-11.title: Iraqis Are Voting for a New Parliament. Here’s What to Know.
2025-11-11.abstract: Iraq is caught in a power struggle between Washington and Tehran, with the Trump administration insisting that the next government disarm powerful Iran-backed militias.

2025-11-11.title: Modi Vows Justice as Police Suspect Terrorism in Deadly New Delhi Blast
2025-11-11.abstract: Those responsible for the explosion “will not be spared,” Prime Minister Narendra Modi of India said. The blast killed at least eight people near a subway station at evening rush hour.

2025-11-12.title: 3 Bob Ross Paintings Sell for $600,000 to Support Public Broadcasters
2025-11-12.abstract: The landscapes by the television host were sold as part of a campaign to help public television stations weather federal funding cuts.

2025-11-12.title: Xi’s Military Purges Show Unease About China’s Nuclear Forces
2025-11-12.abstract: The shake-up in China’s armed forces comes as both Beijing and Washington are pushing through major changes in their country’s militaries, in different ways.

2025-11-12.title: Video Shows the Moment Part of a New Bridge in China Fell
2025-11-12.abstract: A section of the tall bridge in mountainous Sichuan Province fell, apparently after a landslide. No casualties were reported.

2025-11-12.title: Turkish Military Plane Crashes in Georgia, Killing 20 Troops
2025-11-12.abstract: The Turkish defense minister said that 20 Air Force servicemen died when their military cargo plane crashed after taking off from Azerbaijan on Tuesday.

2025-11-12.title: Blood and Tears as Spain’s Troubled Bullfighting Star Hangs Up His Cape
2025-11-12.abstract: José Antonio Morante Camacho says he has fought his last bull. Fans appreciated his artistry but also his honesty about his struggles with mental illness.

2025-11-13.title: Rubio Shrugs Off Allies’ Concerns Over U.S. Drug Strikes
2025-11-13.abstract: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said no one raised the Caribbean military operation in closed-door meetings at a G7 summit. But ministers from France and the European Union publicly called them unlawful.

2025-11-13.title: How France Remembers the November 2015 Terrorist Attacks in Paris
2025-11-13.abstract: A decade ago, Islamic State militants killed 130 people in an assault that shocked France. Some survivors are still struggling, but for many of their compatriots, memories of the attacks are growing more distant.

2025-11-13.title: China’s ‘Wolf Warrior’ Diplomacy Returns With Threat Against Japan’s Leader
2025-11-13.abstract: A Chinese diplomat’s call to cut off the prime minister’s “filthy head” signaled a revival of a combative style Beijing had tried to dial back.

2025-11-13.title: Man Accused of Running Southeast Asia Scam Compound Is Extradited to China
2025-11-13.abstract: China and the United States say She Zhijiang, a Chinese-born businessman, ran a major scam compound in Myanmar. He was arrested in Bangkok in 2022.

2025-11-13.title: Fake Bomb Threats Baffle the Police Across Asia
2025-11-13.abstract: The threatening emails and faxes bear the signature of a man who says he is being impersonated. The police in five nations have failed to stem them.

2025-11-14.title: A.I. Cheating Rattles Top Universities in South Korea
2025-11-14.abstract: As many elite colleges struggle to adapt to the technology, the nation’s most prestigious universities said dozens of students used artificial intelligence tools to cheat.

2025-11-14.title: Kenyan Workers Get Abused Abroad. The President’s Family and Allies Profit.
2025-11-14.abstract: President William Ruto’s government acts as an arm of an industry whose leaders compare women to dogs and blame them for their own abuse, a Times investigation found.

2025-11-14.title: Mexico City Loves Street Food. Its Sewer System Does Not.
2025-11-14.abstract: The worst rainy season in decades caused flooding around much of the city. Fat from its many taco shops, restaurants and markets was a major reason for blocked drains, officials say.

2025-11-14.title: Zelensky Ousted a Heavyweight Mayor. Was It a Power Grab?
2025-11-14.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky removed Odesa’s mayor, raising fears he might be using his wartime powers to tighten control over opposition-run cities.

2025-11-14.title: While Asian Immigrants Work, Burglars Target Their Homes
2025-11-14.abstract: Some law enforcement officials say they think organized crime rings from South America, in particular from Colombia, are responsible for the crime sprees.

2025-11-15.title: Iran Seizes Fuel Tanker on Key Shipping Route
2025-11-15.abstract: The ship, which was flying a Marshall Islands flag, was passing through the Strait of Hormuz when it lost contact with its managers.

2025-11-15.title: 9 Dead After Accidental Blast at Police Station in Kashmir
2025-11-15.abstract: The explosion in the city of Srinagar happened as Indian officials were inspecting bomb-making material seized in an investigation into a terror network allegedly involving doctors.

2025-11-15.title: Russia Tried to Cut Ukraine’s Lights. Now It’s Aiming for the Heat.
2025-11-15.abstract: Moscow’s attacks on gas supplies, the main source of warmth for most Ukrainian households, could plunge millions into the cold.

2025-11-15.title: Trump Organization Is Said to Be in Talks on a Saudi Government Real Estate Deal
2025-11-15.abstract: The chief executive of a Saudi firm says a Trump-branded project is “just a matter of time.” The Trump Organization’s major foreign partner is also signaling new Saudi deals.

2025-11-15.title: Trump Officials Are Policing Words and Foiling Deals at G20 Summit
2025-11-15.abstract: At the annual meeting of the world’s major economic powers, U.S. objections are blocking the usual policy statements, highlighting the president’s distaste for multilateralism — and compromise.

2025-11-16.title: How Many People Die in India From Hot Weather? Nobody Really Knows.
2025-11-16.abstract: Officials have yet to grasp the magnitude of heat-related deaths, let alone effectively deal with the problem, public health experts and scientists say.

2025-11-16.title: Women Toiling in India’s Insufferable Heat Face Mounting Toll on Health
2025-11-16.abstract: Prolonged exposure to hot weather can hinder people’s ability to lead safe and productive lives, experts say.

2025-11-16.title: Women Describe Horrors They Endured in Assad’s Prisons
2025-11-16.abstract: Under the Syrian dictator’s rule, the wives and children of rebels were seized as leverage. Some are now speaking about their trauma.

2025-11-16.title: At a Famed Market, Rats Are Out, Tourists Are In. Is That a Good Thing?
2025-11-16.abstract: A market central to a Brazilian port city’s culture and cuisine got a makeover for the U.N. Climate Change Conference. The results have thrilled some but angered others.

2025-11-16.title: The Unlikely Mother of a Movement, on a Hunger Strike to Avenge Her Son
2025-11-16.abstract: Since her son was killed with 15 others in a railway station collapse in Serbia, Dijana Hrka has become the face of widespread anti-government protests.

2025-11-17.title: Dozens of Australian Schools Close Over Asbestos Fears
2025-11-17.abstract: Three-quarters of the schools in the area around the capital will be closed, although officials say the health risk from asbestos found in colored sand is low.

2025-11-17.title: Trump Gives Legs to South Korea’s Dream for Nuclear-Powered Subs
2025-11-17.abstract: For decades, Washington blocked Seoul’s desire for the attack submarines over concerns about nuclear proliferation. President Trump reversed that policy last month, but left key questions unresolved.

2025-11-17.title: At a Shared Symbol of Jihad, the Taliban Defy Their Old Ties With Pakistan
2025-11-17.abstract: Afghan officials are lashing out against their former allies in the Pakistani military as hostilities between the two countries intensify.

2025-11-17.title: Bangladesh Court Sentences Former Prime Minister to Death
2025-11-17.abstract: Sheikh Hasina, who is in exile in India, was convicted in absentia of crimes against humanity in connection with a bloody crackdown on protests last year.

2025-11-17.title: What Russian Activists Do When They Can’t Criticize Putin
2025-11-17.abstract: Opposition-minded Russians who remain in the country are trying to keep politically active in whatever causes they can, whether that’s recycling, clean air or parking.

2025-11-18.title: Where Mao’s Peasants Tilled the Soil, Tourists Now Pay for the View
2025-11-18.abstract: Decades ago, a Chinese village became an official symbol of revolutionary “self-reliance.” The slogan hasn’t changed, but nearly everything else has.

2025-11-18.title: Tanzania, a Nation Known for Peace, Is Rocked by Deadly Protests
2025-11-18.abstract: Post-election violence has tarnished the country’s reputation for stability, and the crackdown may have backfired on the government, as officials in Washington call for a re-examination of U.S. ties.

2025-11-18.title: Recycling Lead for U.S. Car Batteries Is Poisoning People
2025-11-18.abstract: We documented the toxic fallout of a green technology.

2025-11-18.title: How We Linked the Auto Industry to Lead Poisoning
2025-11-18.abstract: Blood and soil testing confirmed the health consequences. Then we tracked individual shipments to the United States.

2025-11-18.title: Inside the American-Run Base Helping Plan the Future of Gaza
2025-11-18.abstract: U.S. and Israeli soldiers, foreign diplomats and aid workers are congregated in a warehouse in Israel to talk about the future of Gaza. One key group is missing: Palestinians.

2025-11-19.title: Russian Missile Kills at Least 25 in Ukrainian Apartment Building
2025-11-19.abstract: The attack was part of a wave of strikes across the country as President Volodymyr Zelensky seeks to revive stalled peace talks.

2025-11-19.title: Old Divides of East and West Haunt Germany’s Newest Arrivals
2025-11-19.abstract: Refugees who poured into the country a decade ago fared better when they landed in the more prosperous West, an enduring legacy of reunification.

2025-11-19.title: The Climate Is Changing, Cows Are Stressed and Italy’s Cheesemakers Are Worried
2025-11-19.abstract: Extreme heat causes cows to produce less milk. This poses problems in cheese-obsessed Italy, especially as it tries to meet growing demand for favorites like burrata.

2025-11-19.title: Zelensky Under Siege as Corruption Case Shatters Ukraine’s Wartime Unity
2025-11-19.abstract: An anti-Zelensky political coalition is coalescing as the president’s allies are accused of enriching themselves while the country’s soldiers die on the battlefield.

2025-11-19.title: Two Miss Universe Judges Resign Before Finale, Deepening a Crisis
2025-11-19.abstract: One accused the competition of being rigged. It is the second scandal to roil the pageant after an organizer berated a contestant in public.

2025-11-20.title: The Hunt Is On for Bears in Japan After Deadly Attacks
2025-11-20.abstract: Residents in the northern region of Akita are living in fear after dozens of assaults on humans. Riot police and the army have been sent to help.

2025-11-20.title: Franco Died 50 Years Ago, but He’s Still Winning New Fans in Spain
2025-11-20.abstract: Young Spaniards are increasingly drawn to the dictator Francisco Franco. So the government is designing apps, games and T-shirts to promote democracy.

2025-11-20.title: Mount Semeru Erupts and Rains Ash on Villages
2025-11-20.abstract: Mount Semeru on the island of Java is one of Indonesia’s most active volcanoes. Its latest eruption forced hundreds to flee their homes.

2025-11-20.title: Ex-Mayor Is Sentenced to Life for Human Trafficking Tied to Scam Centers
2025-11-20.abstract: A court in the Philippines convicted Alice Guo for trafficking people to a compound that officials have linked to online scams and organized crime.

2025-11-20.title: Ferry Crashed After Operator Was Distracted by His Phone, Officials Say
2025-11-20.abstract: The operator and two other officers were charged with gross negligence after the ferry ran aground in South Korea on Wednesday carrying 267 people.

2025-11-21.title: Hundreds of Invasive Goldfish to Be Culled From Ottawa Pond
2025-11-21.abstract: Thousands washed ashore dead in the spring, but the fish are back. “They’re pretty hard to kill,” a biology professor said.

2025-11-21.title: Australia Adds Twitch to Social Media Ban for Teens
2025-11-21.abstract: Twitch, a livestreaming service popular with gamers, joins nine other platforms that will be required to remove Australian users under the age of 16.

2025-11-21.title: Researcher’s Smuggling Arrest Casts Light on Dispute Over Chinese Students
2025-11-21.abstract: As some lawmakers press U.S. universities to curtail ties with China, a postdoctoral student’s prosecution raises questions about how big the danger actually is.

2025-11-21.title: Desperate Search for Missing Syrians Leads to Mass Graves
2025-11-21.abstract: More than 100,000 people disappeared during the civil war. To bring closure to some of their families, the new government faces the challenging task of exhuming remains from scores of burial sites.

2025-11-21.title: Survivors Describe Deadly Snowstorm in Chilean Patagonia
2025-11-21.abstract: Five tourists, from Mexico, Britain and Germany, died Monday on a popular hiking trail in the Torres del Paine National Park.

2025-11-22.title: Trump Says Violence Against Christians in Nigeria Is ‘Genocide.’ It’s Not So Simple.
2025-11-22.abstract: There are widespread attacks across the country affecting many religious and ethnic groups. Many of them defy a simple explanation.

2025-11-22.title: What Is Trump’s 28-Point Plan to End Russia’s War in Ukraine?
2025-11-22.abstract: President Trump is pressing Kyiv to accept an outcome that yields to President Vladimir V. Putin’s demands.

2025-11-22.title: Russian Exiles See Europe Visa Rule as Blow Against Them, Not Putin
2025-11-22.abstract: The European Union said the decision was prompted by sabotage attacks in Europe, but Russians living abroad say Europe is punishing ordinary people.

2025-11-22.title: Germans Are Going Off Beer. That’s Forcing Brewers to Adapt or Go Bust.
2025-11-22.abstract: More young people are steering clear of alcohol. The deepening cultural shift has spawned an epidemic of brewery closures.

2025-11-22.title: On These South African Farms, Black and White Neighbors Fight Crime Together
2025-11-22.abstract: An audacious effort joined two disparate communities, who say they all suffer from the staggering violence and crime affecting the country.

2025-11-23.title: In Russian-Occupied Mariupol, Everything Ukrainian Must Go
2025-11-23.abstract: Russia is remaking Mariupol, which was devastated by a brutal siege in 2022. Ukrainians seeking to move back are finding it hard to recognize the city, or to reclaim their property.

2025-11-23.title: Ukraine and U.S. Cite Progress in Talks on Ending War With Russia
2025-11-23.abstract: President Trump lashed out at Ukraine even as the talks on his peace proposal were still taking place, accusing the country’s leadership of being ungrateful for American support.

2025-11-23.title: Democracy Is in Trouble. This Region Is Turning to Its People.
2025-11-23.abstract: A small corner of Belgium is recruiting ordinary citizens to help create policies. Participants say it’s renewed their faith in government.

2025-11-23.title: Haitians Rejoice Over Their First World Cup Berth in 50 Years
2025-11-23.abstract: The national team’s success in landing a World Cup slot gave Haitians around the world a momentary reprieve from their country’s deep crises.

2025-11-23.title: Titanic Passenger’s Pocket Watch Sells for $2.3 Million
2025-11-23.abstract: The watch belonged to Isidor Straus, a co-owner of Macy’s who was traveling first class on the Titanic with his wife when it sank in April 1912.

2025-11-24.title: As Ukraine Sets ‘Red Lines,’ a U.S. Peace Plan Is Slimmed Down
2025-11-24.abstract: Washington and Kyiv said that “highly productive” discussions over a proposal to end the war between Russia and Ukraine would continue.

2025-11-24.title: Malaysia to Bar Children Under 16 From Social Media, Echoing Australian Ban
2025-11-24.abstract: The announcement on Sunday, which was light on details, came weeks before a similar action takes effect in Australia.

2025-11-24.title: Before Berlin’s Cowboys Are Booted Off Their Land, One Final Hoedown
2025-11-24.abstract: The town looks straight out of the Wild West, with saloons, sheriffs and cowboy hats galore, but in this endangered patch of the American frontier, everyone is speaking German.

2025-11-24.title: Russian Disinformation Comes to Mexico, Seeking to Rupture U.S. Ties
2025-11-24.abstract: A U.S. government cable said that Kremlin-run outlets had scaled up their efforts across Latin America, seeking to turn people against the United States and garner support for Russia.

2025-11-24.title: Vietnam’s Year of Floods, Mud and Death
2025-11-24.abstract: Scientists suggested that climate change could make central Vietnam a global hot spot for destructive storms. This year has seemed to prove the point.

2025-11-25.title: ‘Find a Job,’ Ontario Premier Tells Protester While Passing a New Housing Law
2025-11-25.abstract: During a raucous hearing, the Ontario legislature passed a bill backed by Premier Doug Ford meant to expedite housing development and the eviction of tenants who are behind on their rent.

2025-11-25.title: Acclaimed Writer of Indigenous Stories Says He Is Not Cherokee
2025-11-25.abstract: Thomas King said he felt “ripped in half” on learning he had no Indigenous ancestry. The Canadian author has dedicated his career to writing about Indigenous people.

2025-11-25.title: Warmed by Japan’s Support, Taiwan Takes Up Sushi Diplomacy
2025-11-25.abstract: China closed off Japanese seafood imports after Japan’s new leader declared strong support for Taiwan. Suddenly, sushi is everywhere on Taiwanese social media.

2025-11-25.title: Russia Strikes Ukraine and Signals Resistance to Amended Peace Plan
2025-11-25.abstract: The attack killed at least seven people in Kyiv, the authorities said, as Moscow suggested it would resist changes negotiated by Ukraine.

2025-11-25.title: Australian Senator Suspended for Burqa-Wearing Stunt in Parliament
2025-11-25.abstract: Pauline Hanson, the head of the far-right One Nation party, wore the garment as a prop to push legislation to bar it in public places.

2025-11-26.title: The New Putin Calendar Is Here: ‘A Man for Every Season’
2025-11-26.abstract: January shows the Russian leader astride a snowmobile. For February, he flips a judo partner. In August, he offers advice: “My recipe for energy: Sleep little, work a lot and don’t whine.”

2025-11-26.title: Taiwan’s President to Seek an Extra $40 Billion for Military
2025-11-26.abstract: President Lai Ching-te says the spending, which must be approved by the island’s legislature, would fund purchases of arms from the United States.

2025-11-26.title: What to Know About Pope Leo’s First Trip as Pontiff
2025-11-26.abstract: The pope chose a region rife with religious divisions and conflict for his first trip abroad. He arrived in Lebanon on Sunday after a few days in Turkey.

2025-11-26.title: ‘You Start Getting Desperate’: How It Feels to Be Young and Jobless in Britain
2025-11-26.abstract: Rising youth unemployment is one of the challenges that will affect the success of the British chancellor’s economic approach as she unveils a crucial budget on Wednesday.

2025-11-26.title: Spat With China Becomes an Asset for Japan’s New Leader
2025-11-26.abstract: Japan’s prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, is not backing off from comments about Taiwan that enraged China. Many of Japan’s voters like her stance.

2025-11-27.title: Hong Kong Arrests 3 Tied to Construction Company After Deadly Fire
2025-11-27.abstract: The police said that building materials used by the company for the work at Wang Fuk Court may not have been up to fire safety standards. The police did not identify the company or who was arrested.

2025-11-27.title: Heading to the Mideast, Pope Leo May Show ‘Who He Really Is’
2025-11-27.abstract: The pontiff begins a trip to Turkey and Lebanon on Thursday — the first foreign voyage of his papacy, and his biggest test yet.

2025-11-27.title: In Turkey With a Message of Outreach, Pope Meets Erdogan
2025-11-27.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan received Leo XIV on the opening leg of a trip that will also include Lebanon.

2025-11-27.title: Trump Cut Europe Out of Ukraine Talks. Here’s How Europe Pushed Back.
2025-11-27.abstract: European leaders were blindsided by President Trump’s 28-point-plan to end the Ukraine war, setting off a dash for influence.

2025-11-28.title: These Scam Centers Were Blown Up. Was It All for Show?
2025-11-28.abstract: Myanmar’s junta made a grand display of demolishing buildings that hosted the centers, even broadcasting the explosions. But the scammers have found new homes.

2025-11-28.title: In Turkey, Pope Seeks to Soothe an Ancient Christian Divide
2025-11-28.abstract: In Istanbul, Pope Leo XIV will meet the patriarch of the Eastern Orthodox Church to show amity between two of the world’s largest Christian groups.

2025-11-28.title: Sri Lanka Battered by Cyclone as Death Toll Climbs to 69
2025-11-28.abstract: Torrential downpours and high winds struck large parts of the island nation as Cyclone Ditwah churned northwest toward India.

2025-11-28.title: Death Toll Rises to 128 in Hong Kong High-Rise Fire
2025-11-28.abstract: Hope of finding survivors has dwindled, with many residents of the Wang Fuk Court towers still unaccounted for. Eight more people were arrested Friday over the blaze.

2025-11-28.title: Home of Ukraine’s Lead Negotiator Is Searched in Corruption Case
2025-11-28.abstract: The scrutiny of Andriy Yermak, President Volodymyr Zelensky’s chief of staff, could disrupt U.S.-led peace talks and further rattle Ukrainian politics.

2025-11-29.title: Where Hundreds of Undocumented Migrants Have Died in Custody
2025-11-29.abstract: Malaysia launched a “year of enforcement” in response to a surge in undocumented migrants, many of them from Myanmar. Some faced nightmarish fates.

2025-11-29.title: Lured by Jobs, They Ended Up ‘Going to War’ for Russia
2025-11-29.abstract: The South African government is investigating how more than a dozen men unwittingly ended up on the front line in Russia’s war on Ukraine.

2025-11-29.title: Deadly Hong Kong Fire Is a Test of Beijing’s Rule in the City
2025-11-29.abstract: After Beijing reshaped the political order in Hong Kong in its image, the fire has become a test of how well that new system can govern in a crisis.

2025-11-29.title: As Cyclone Deaths Pass 150, Sri Lanka Is Overwhelmed by Rescue Demand
2025-11-29.abstract: Rescue efforts across the nation of 23 million have been hampered by disruptions in transport and telecommunications.

2025-11-29.title: Russia Bombards Ukraine for Nearly 10 Hours in a Deadly Assault
2025-11-29.abstract: The attack came as U.S. officials were expected to hold peace talks with Ukrainian and Russian officials in the coming days.

2025-11-30.title: Fed Up With the Taliban, Pakistan Expels One Million Afghans
2025-11-30.abstract: Labeling Afghans a national security threat, Pakistan has intensified expulsions this year, depriving people of a haven from Afghanistan’s turmoil.

2025-11-30.title: Trump-Endorsed Candidate and Sportscaster Lead Honduran Presidential Race
2025-11-30.abstract: Electoral authorities said partial results showed the two conservative candidates were virtually tied — and far ahead of the governing left-wing party.

2025-11-30.title: Christians in Lebanon Look to Pope Leo’s Visit for a Message of Hope and Peace
2025-11-30.abstract: The pontiff met political leaders upon his arrival on Sunday and will later say Mass at a Beirut port destroyed by an explosion five years ago.

2025-11-30.title: India Is a Rising Power, but Breathing in Its Capital Is Hazardous
2025-11-30.abstract: With little being done to clean the skies over New Delhi, the huge city struggles with toxic air.

2025-11-30.title: Deadly Floods in Indonesia Leave Hundreds Missing
2025-11-30.abstract: Hundreds of people have been killed and millions displaced as extreme weather has ravaged Southeast Asia this month. Indonesia’s heavy rain was linked to two tropical cyclones.
